privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only; no raw chat, health, pet-health, credentials, contact data, account identifiers, or unreviewed uploads.

memory_slice.title: Pinned means immutable reference, not trusted or successfully executed dependency

records.summary: Cloudflare Research adopted reviewed 40-character GitHub Action commit pins and replaced mutable major-version tags in the deployment security gate with exact commit references.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch both commits and verify that each workflow reference equals the reviewed manifest SHA rather than a mutable tag.

records.summary: Within the project security vocabulary, 'pinned' must be interpreted narrowly as resistance to later tag movement. It must not be used as a synonym for safe, trusted, compatible, audited, executed, or passing.
supersedes: Any prior shorthand that treated immutable pinning as complete dependency verification
records.falsification_or_review_route: Reject this inference only if a later explicit project decision broadens the term and supplies separate evidence for source audit, dependency audit, compatibility, execution, and successful CI.

records.summary: Future evidence packets should report dependency state as separate fields: reference_immutable, source_identity_reviewed, upstream_audited, transitive_dependencies_audited, runtime_compatible, workflow_executed, and workflow_passed. No field may imply another.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Test the rule against future queue and evidence records; revise it if distinct states cannot be determined independently or if an authoritative schema defines stronger semantics.

records.summary: The inspected commits establish a reviewed manifest and exact pins in one security-gate workflow, but do not establish that every workflow uses approved immutable references, that transitive dependencies were audited, or that the pinned workflow executed successfully on the canonical branch.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Enumerate all workflow uses entries at the canonical commit, compare them with the reviewed manifest, inspect transitive dependency review evidence, and retain commit-bound GitHub Actions results.
